publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-01-16 23:46:49 -0500.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Files relating to John Guckenheimer's research and teaching. Includes correspondence, grant files, information on Cornell committees, files relating to the Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics (SIAM).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Guckenheimer papers, #14-19-4264. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Guckenheimer worked at the University of California at Santa Cruz before joining the Cornell University Department of Mathematics in 1985 and was later appointed the Abram R. Bullis Professor of Mathematics. He also served as Associate Dean for Scientific Computing in the Department of Computing and Information Science. He was elected President of the Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics. He was a Guggenheim and American Mathematical Society fellow and a Leroy P. Steele Prize winner. His research areas included neuroscience, algorithms for periodic orbits, and dynamics in systems with multiple time scales.

Series V. Research, publications, and related correspondence
Publication: Bifurcation of the Hodgkin and Huxley Equations: A New Twist

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bifurcation & Catastrophe; Review of "When Time Breaks Down" by Arthur Winfree; Sensitive Dependence to Initial Conditions for One Dimensional Maps; Renormalization of One Dimensional Mappings and Strange Attractors; The Mathematical Intelligencer 1978

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Monotone Equivalence and Topological Equivalence; the Catastrophe Controversy; A Codimension Two Bifurcation with Circular Symmetry
